name: Go see Elena at the Guild counter.

theScene: According to my records, you got the Sigil of Allure! Congratulations! You're already well on your way to defeating the Demon Queen!
The next sigil is inside a temple that sits atop the mountain at the edge of the forest. The grimoire should already have the mountain added to your map.
You're going to have to go up the mountain to get there, but thankfully there's a magic-powered lift, so you won't have to take a harrowing journey to climb the whole thing.
Unless the lift breaks. Again. I {i}really{/i} hope it doesn't. It takes so much paperwork to get that fixed, it feels like it takes months to fill it all out...
Elena trails off, muttering something about shoving the paperwork up a knight's ass, and some other things you don't quite hear.
According to my records, you reached the top of the mountain! Congratulations! At least the lift didn't get destroyed this time! {i}Right?♪{/i}
You stare at her for a quiet moment unsure how to break the news to her, and Elena's brief flicker of businesslike cheer wilts away.
It... It got destroyed again, didn't it?
You nod, and she's left looking completely in despair, despondent to the point where you don't think she can even cry...
Goddess, why? Every {i}single{/i} time someone goes to use it... The paperwork...

W-well i-it's not {i}all{/i} bad, hehe... at {i}least{/i} you can enter the next dungeon now... So go and try to get the sigil of Will-Power, just be sure to check Amber's shop before you go, the dungeon is all about the mind and your strength, so maybe get some equipment to help with that.
Her forced smile fades away back to her usual curt expression, the light in her eyes still notably faded.
I think... I think I'm going to get something to drink once work is over... Maybe a {i}lot{/i} of somethings...
She stares off into the distance with a look of despair on her face. You're pretty sure she's not planning on the fun kind of drinking...
According to my records, you got the Sigil of Will-Power! Congratulations! It's estimated that only 75% of adventurers on Lucidia actually get this far, so be proud of yourself.
Aside from the occasional monstrification of a woman, nothing really bad tends to happen to the other 25%, they simply give up trying to beat the temple, or never reach it in the first place.
Usually they just focus on making money where they can to pay off their debt, or cozy up with some monster girl they met on the way, or some fellow adventurer.
Elena almost sounds wistful as she sighs before she puts up her businesslike smile again.

But getting to the next sigil is a real challenge for adventurers, you need to trek through dimly lit caverns under the mountain and then through a well-guarded labyrinth to get to the monster girl Capital, which holds the sigil of luck.

Though I'm getting ahead of myself, you need to take this one step at a time. The caverns are full of all kinds of predatory monsters, many of which have paralytic abilities. So be sure to stock up at Amber's shop for supplies to deal with that.

Trust me, you're going to want to get stocked up before you go in there. The Caverns and Labyrinth only have around a 50% clear rate for adventurers. Though most of them just get scared off by the more 'diverse' monsters in there and head back to the tamer areas.
Or they vanish for weeks into the depths of that place so I have to fill out an extensive M.I.A. paperwork. Only for them to {i}waltz{/i} out later with their new {i}wife.{/i}
While she {i}seems{/i} disgusted, the tone of her voice comes off as more pouty and jealous... Before her thoughts slip to something else.

The worst case of all though is at the very least very rare, when the person who went in stays in there because they got hooked on spending time with {i}toxic{/i} matangos, thinking they're in {i}love{/i}. We have freaking signs marking where they are.
She lets out an exasperated sigh as she continues to go off on her minor tangential rant.
I've seen too many cases of crippling heartbreak thanks to those manipulative fungal bitches that I don't even care about the paperwork for it. I'm just sad and angry for the person they hurt.
She lets out a stressed sigh as she takes a moment to decompress, quietly rebuilding her business face.

Well, I hope that exemplifies that you should stay away from toxic matangos, or at the very least understand they really do {i}not{/i} care for you... Ugh, well I guess I've seen at least one case where they actually ended up a cute couple, in a clingy, creepy as hell kind of way...
...You know, it's also really weird that the women they monstrify don't become other matangos, I thought that was how matangos worked...
Elena is mostly muttering to herself at this point and puts on a smile as she brings her focus back to you.
Anyways, good luck adventuring out there. Remember if you get helplessly lost in the caverns your recall stone can get you out of there still.
Oh right! The guild is now able to offer you its respec services. Ask me about it if you're interested.
According to my records you've reached the Labyrinth! Congratulations! Next, you just need to navigate through the twisting halls of the labyrinth and avoid its dreaded guardians.
After that, you'll reach the monster girl Capital. Which is arguably far more dangerous than any place you've been to before. The entire population of single monsters will be after you there.
But first things first, the Labyrinth, don't let your ego tell you what to do, just run from the guardians there. Your pelvis will thank you...
Other than that I don't really have any tips to give you, Amber might sell you a few things to help you run away from them, but most of it's going to be up to you.
According to my records, you've passed through the Labyrinth successfully and acquired your Lucidian Passport! Congratulations!
Her business smile fades after a flickering moment and a heavy sigh.
You know... ...I don't get to say that very often...
She takes a moment to clear her throat, lifting her clenched hand just shy of her soft lips, her bust visibly jiggling from the act, before her face lights up with more genuine joy.
Her sparkling energetic cheer with one arm gesturing in a wide arc makes you jump from the sudden exuberance of the normally businesslike woman.
Elena then readjusts her posture with a satisfied smile, smoothing out the creases in her uniform.
{i}There.{/i} That seemed like proper praise for that achievement. Now then...
Her smile returns to a more prim and proper one, though her voice remains as suitably cheery.
Your next step will be to explore the dreaded monster girl Capital. It is likely most of the local population will be after you in some way, though I hear the place is also quite the wonder...
But seriously, at least {i}try{/i} to stay on task while you're there... Although I've never been there, so there isn't much intel I can actually give you...
She lets out a moody sigh as she mulls something over, her expression practically yelling she wants a vacation or something before she perks up back to her professional cheer.
There's a slight pause before a slightly eager smile slips onto the tall woman's soft lips.
...Though- say- you managed to keep up with hellhounds right-? Do you think-
She catches herself before she can finish her train of thought or you can answer, and clears her throat, her gaze a clear suggestion to forget about what you just heard as she gives a pointed cough.
{i}Ahem-{/i} nevermind- right- keep it professional. {i}So{/i} is there anything the guild can help you with today?
The guildhall is effectively empty right now, so you walk easily over to a very bored looking Elena at the guild counter, and she gives you a business-like smile.

You mill about the guild hall for a moment as Elena finishes chatting with another adventurer about a quest they just completed. Then you head over to the counter once her business is concluded and she gives you a calm smile.
There's a brief lineup of people Elena has to deal with before you get to talk to her, apparently there was an issue with some quest postings, but once she's free she gives you a collected smile.

As you approach the guild counter Elena is in the middle of doing a pile of paperwork with practiced efficiency and the enthusiasm of a rock. As she sees you coming near a genuine smile spreads over her face as she gets an excuse to take a break.
As you reach the guild counter Elena is just finishing up the last of her paperwork, only for a new pile to materialize in its place. Leaving her sighing as she looks to you with a forced smile.

You navigate past an assortment of other adventurers chatting to each other as you make your way over to the guild counter, and you interrupt Elena from doing her idle paperwork, making her give you a small smile as you distract her from the monotony.

As you get closer to the guild counter you see Elena idly playing with a metal pen in one hand as she gazes down at her paperwork, but her glare tightens with annoyance and she accidentally snaps the pen in two with one finger, causing her to notice you, so she hurriedly hides the remains in her desk and gives you a clearly fake smile.

You head over to Elena as she counts eros and tosses into pouches before setting the pouches aside or into drawers behind her desk, as you get closer she gives you a businesslike smile and set aside her quest reward preparation to talk to you.
Ask for guidance on adventuring. (Detailed Tutorial Information.)
Step away from the guild counter.
Where in the world did you {i}find{/i} that?!
Actually, nevermind, I'm sure there's still a few of them floating around from when the guild did that promotion...

But in short, that ticket will let you use a 'respec' tome, to help you re-adjust your abilities, for free. Normally it's quite expensive, and you'll even be able to use the service even if you wouldn't normally be able to.
Of course you don't have to use it. It would let you redo any of your training up to this point though.
Elena lets out a deep sigh, but gives you an all business smile as she continues to speak.
Well, I suppose that means you get to respec for free again if you want.
Oh, it seems like you don't have enough money for the respec. Feel free to come back later when you have it.

It's... Complicated? Elly would be able to explain it better, but basically a respec tome works like a skill tome, but in reverse? Like, it takes all the training you've done and makes it into this kind of primordial junk you can easily apply elsewhere.

Of course a process like this isn't exactly simple, and the more times you do it, the more complex it gets, so you'll need a better made respec tome every time. Personally I advocate sticking to your training and life choices, rather than flip flopping on it.
Elena cheerfully takes the ticket from you and passes you a respec tome, before cathartically ripping up the silver ticket with a smile.
You wouldn't believe all the work these things caused me and Elly...
As you open the respec tome a tingling spreads through your body and mind. (Shift can be used to increment stats by 5.)
I hope you're happy with your choices [ThePlayerName]. Let me know if you want to respec again.
Just uh... Don't do it too often, okay? I hear it can really mess with your head...
Are you sure you want to respec [ThePlayerName]? Keep in mind every time you do it, it's going to cost more money. At least until it's absurdly expensive.
You hand Elena the money, and she passes you a rather plain looking book, that once you open it sends magic tingling through your mind and body... (Shift can be used to increment stats by 5.)
I hope you're happy with your choices [ThePlayerName]. Let me know if you want to respec again.
Just uh... Don't do it too often, okay? I hear it can really mess with your head...

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask for guidance on adventuring. (Detailed Tutorial Information.)

theScene: Talk about the guild and adventuring.
Talk about other people in town.

EventText.NameOfScene: Talk about the guild and adventuring.
theScene: How do your records keep so up to date on my progress?
Shouldn't there be more people working here?
Do you teach any normal combat skills?
Isn't that outfit a bit too revealing for a guild uniform?

EventText.NameOfScene: How do your records keep so up to date on my progress?
theScene: Oh, well just like how the grimoires are connected to the quest board, my records are connected to all the grimoires, so they automatically keep tabs on how far you've gotten, which sigils you've acquired, and quests you've completed.
She gives you a very flat and level stare.
Before you ask, no the records of the guild don't track anything personal such as preferred grimoire setups or anything similar to 'combat' stats or track your interactions with monsters.
Even if they {i}did{/i} track any of that, there is no way in hell I would read any of it.
That aside the system just helps make my job easier when it comes to keeping tabs on adventurers and ensuring rewards and congratulations are properly handed out to people.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.
What are eros? They're {i}money{/i} of course.
Her rather smug tone catches you a bit off guard and she gently laughs as she gives you a smile.
I'm kidding, well I mean they {i}are{/i} money, but I knew what you meant.
I'd recommend you ask Elly if you want a more detailed description of what they're made from. It's a lot of magic stuff.
Elena dismissively waves her hand at the end of her statement, her smile slipping into a more serious frown as she continues.

But basically eros are crystallized {i}sex{/i} energy. Kind of. They don't break the economy because they actually get used to make and enchant things, and some monsters just straight up {i}eat{/i} them.
I can assure you they're perfectly sanitary and more crystallized energy than sex-related. After all, they're not made every time people have sex.

You would think I'd complain about what they're made of compared to gold coins, but Eros is just so {i}convenient.{/i} You can easily combine coins into higher-value coins and it tracks the value {i}on{/i} the coin, they even do it automatically when you go to get money. How? {i}Magic.{/i} Again, ask Elly if you want more detail.
Wait, if eros combine like that why do you hand out bags full of coins?
Because it's way more satisfying to get a bag full of money than just one coin with all the money printed on it.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.

EventText.NameOfScene: Shouldn't there be more people working here?
theScene: I've asked myself that question before, the answer is always yes, there should be more people working at a guildhall.
Oh, how I wish there were more people to split the work between, or more ways to automate things, trust me Elly has tried to automate the paperwork and failed...
As for {i}why{/i} it's just the two of us. It's a combination of budgetary constraints and location.
At the very least the two of us get paid very well, and thanks to Elly's magic the cleaning and a bunch of other boring junk is dealt with easily.
Though I do wish I could hire extra help, but I'm contractually obligated to not, or I lose my job.
She lets out a tired sigh as she continues.
Honestly this job isn't {i}that{/i} bad, it's just so {i}boring{/i} and just wears away at you.
Elena then lets out a wistful sigh.

Really makes me wish I passed up the job to be a guild-master... I just thought it would be like a {i}normal{/i} promotion. Suppose I should have listened to Elly more instead of just rushing forward. Next thing I knew, I was shipped off here on a new, {i}brave{/i} frontier, pushing paperwork...
You think you see her eye twitch as she recalls the memory...
Talk about the guild and adventuring.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.

EventText.NameOfScene: Do you teach any normal combat skills?
theScene: I mean, they're all useless here, so other than something me or Elly could personally teach you, nope. We only have skill tomes for {i}'practical'{/i} skills.
You nod, recalling the magic tomes that allow you to almost instantly learn a technique or spell provided you have the potential to do so, as well as the occasional personal lesson being offered.
Heh, honestly I'm just glad I don't have to teach every single adventurer who comes in here how to have {i}sex.{/i} Elly even takes the time to make basically all of the skill tomes we offer here.
I can really only make the more standard skill tomes for healing and buffing spells anyways, making those things is a bit too delicate for me. It's nice having such a trusty Archmage.♪
Elena takes a moment to lean back as she stretches lazily, making her bountiful cleavage bounce temptingly right in your line of sight before Elena lets out a sigh.
Though maybe I'll offer to teach you in private sometime if you're interested.
She lightly coughs and brushes her outfit with a hand.
Her earlier more flirty expression suggests otherwise despite her now more businesslike demeanor.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.

EventText.NameOfScene: Isn't that outfit a bit too revealing for a guild uniform?
theScene: Elena gives you a look of confusion as she leans one of her elbows on the counter, incidentally resting her ample breasts on the surface and emphasizing her cleavage.
Huh? What do you mean my outfit's too revealing for a guild unif-

Realization clearly hits her as a smug and suggestive smile spreads over her face, and she rests her other elbow on the counter and sets her chin on her overlaid hands, framing her delightful soft valley of tempting skin.
{i}Oh{/i} you mean how my outfit shows off my legs, hips, and {i}breasts{/i} like this?
Elena gently shakes her clothed tits while they rest on the counter, making it very hard for your eyes not to be drawn to her bursting cleavage.

See the funny thing is, as a guild-master I actually get to wear whatever outfit I want as long as it meets 'professional standards'. So don't go thinking I'm forced to wear this and {i}look{/i} all you want.
She stands back up to her full height with a more businesslike smile as she speaks with a danger-laced tone.
Just don't try and touch me unsolicited, you'll wish I was able to just punch you for recompense instead.
Her almost threatening smile slips back to one of suggestive smugness as she mulls something over.
You know, all this talk about my outfit reminds me of the {i}skimpy{/i} metal bikini I used to wear while adventuring.
She sighs wistfully, watching your reaction to the mental image she gave you. Leaving you to change the topic.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.
Talk about the guild and adventuring.
So you used to be an adventurer?
You clearly don't like working here, so why not quit?
I do {i}not{/i} have a drinking problem.
There's an awkward silence for a moment and you hear Elly loudly cough from her reading table.
I just get that a lot, sorry.♪
Elena does her best to try and smoothly move the conversation on with her cheerful smile and happy tone.

{i}Anyways,{/i} I wouldn't even really say I have a drinking {i}habit{/i} so much as I just really like to unwind after a hard day of work, drink a bit to celebrate something, or drink to forget a horrible date.
So just put aside any silly rumors about me being some kind of rampant alcoholic that you may have heard around town.

Besides it's not like I can even get drunk off anything not top-shelf, magically enhanced, or made with monster brewing techniques and materials. I'm just too strong to get affected by anything minor. So drinking is a little bit expensive for me anyway.
Makes me a little glad for the size of my paycheck, to be honest.
Though I can't deny that I'm at least a {i}little{/i} bit of an alcohol connoisseur, and the drinks at Vivian's Inn are frankly {i}amazing.♪{/i}
Sure most of them are spiked with aphrodisiacs of some kind, but I'd hardly call that much of a problem, especially if I had {i}someone{/i} to go drink with, you know?
She lets the suggestion hang for a moment before a brimming smile takes her face as she starts to gush about the drinks there.
Seriously though, the selection, the taste, the sheer quality, are all {i}perfect.{/i} I even helped them come up with a few new house drinks.♪
Elena finally manages to curb her shining enthusiasm enough to bring herself back to a normal business smile.
Ahem. So was there anything else you wanted to talk about?

EventText.NameOfScene: So you used to be an adventurer?
theScene: Hm? Oh yeah! Back on Fertilia me and Elly were adventurers for... Honestly, I don't know how long. Subduing bandits and evildoers, hunting down old relics, looting ruins, tons of stuff!♪
Now I work a desk job. {i}Yay.{/i}
The mocking enthusiasm in her voice is jarring compared to her prior excitement, but her smile returns as she continues after a pause.
But I bitch about paperwork all the time, I bet you asked because you were curious about my adventurous past.

Hmm, let's see... Stopped so many bandits I've lost count, I think Elly still remembers, uhhh, we stopped a doomsday cult one time. Though they were clearly off their rockers because they planned to just throw chickens at people or something.
You hear Elly pipe up from her corner of the guild office stating 'It was goats.'
Oh right! It {i}was{/i} goats they were catapulting at people. Where did I get chickens from... The chicken girl bandits we knocked out? Nah... Anyways!
We did slay a dragon.♪ Well, okay we didn't {i}slay{/i} her. I just smacked her upside the head until she agreed to stop pillaging and burning down towns. Slay just sounds way more impressive.
She sighs with happy memories, but slowly shifts to a slight frown.
Thinking about it makes me itch for a proper fight, to be honest. Here it's all sex, sex, sex.
Don't get me wrong, I've got nothing against sex.
It's just not the same as the clash of battle you know? Nor do the feats really sound as impressive.
She then sighs wistfully and leans on her counter.
The more I think about it, the more I miss the thrill of battle, to be honest... Ugh, now I want a drink...
You think you hear Elly mutter something about 'Battle maniac.', but you can't quite be sure.

EventText.NameOfScene: You clearly don't like working here, so why not quit?
theScene: Oh, well, that's a whole tangle of different issues really. Quitting means they would have to find an all-new guild master to run the place, and Elly would be stuck running it all alone without me here and I don't think she could handle that well. I mean, sure we could both quit, but she actually doesn't mind it here.

I also don't think I can trust someone else to actually run this place, to be honest. At least not right now. I'm sure plenty of weirdos would gun for this position, that or some fanatical wackjob or something.

The bigger issue for me though would be that if I quit being a guild master, I'd have to quit the guild entirely and getting back in after leaving can take {i}years{/i}, especially after being tasked with something so important. Not to mention having to get promotions all over again, the sheer number of tests, and the scrutiny. For all they know if you quit, it's because you went turncoat or something.
And I'd be lying if I said I didn't like the money. I can basically afford any weapon, booze, armor, you name it. As long as I'm not completely stupid with my money at least.

Though the job is definitely stressful, trying to keep track of all these people, give advice, try and steer people away from bad decisions, do my best to console people about whatever... it's hard. Especially if I fuck up.
She lets out a tired sigh but her face doesn't slip to anything more than a mild frown.

Really, paperwork just gets to me because it's {i}so{/i} boring and monotonous. It makes me just want to run out there and {i}hit{/i} something. Sparring would be amazing, but you know, enchantment that prevents violence and all that. So I just drink a little bit more than normal. I'll be fine.

{i}What!{/i} Who told you I have dating issues. I don't have dating {i}issues.{/i} It was {i}Lillian{/i} wasn't it! Confidential confessional booth my ass- Wait I bet it was {i}Vivian.{/i} She's always sticking her nose into other people's love lives.
Elena practically hisses out her response with a hint of rage, before it turns to despondence.
No wait, it was probably Elly who told you I have romance problems... Probably by accident...
Elena lets out a tired sigh and Elly pipes up from her seat in the corner of the guild.
S-Sister, you're starting to make a scene.
A smile spreads over Elena's face as she perks up to respond to her sister.
I am not going to make a scene Elly, I'm going to defend my dating {i}honor{/i} is what I'm going to do.
Elly sighs in exasperation and shoves her face back into her book muttering hopelessly.

First off [ThePlayerName], I'm sure you've heard some baseless accusations that I'm some kind of, {i}'rampant alcoholic'{/i}. I can assure you I drink a perfectly {i}normal{/i} amount for a woman of my stature, strength, and daily stress level.
Elly pokes her head up from behind her book and glares at her sister.
Sister, you get completely wasted every night. That is not healthy nor attractive behavior.
Daily {i}stress{/i} level Elly. Just because I can drink an oni under the table doesn't mean I have a problem.
Elly sighs despondently and goes back to her book, you get the feeling this isn't the first time they've had this conversation...

Secondly, it's not {i}my{/i} fault that so many men are afraid of a woman who could break them in two with one hand. There's nothing wrong with a guy being helplessly pinned by a woman, I mean sure it's a little shocking given my lack of visible muscle.
Seriously I can't get abs to save my life, no clue why. It really kind of a pisses me off sometimes- I'm getting off track.
Anyways! The point is, there's nothing wrong if a guy ends up the bottom in the relationship.
Elena clears her throat a little, and Elly slumps in her chair. Did the guildhall clear out at some point? Or was it this empty when you got here...

Oh right. It is true though. Any oni or minotaur would agree. Just don't compare me to one. That's just kind of rude, alright? I'm not some kind of {i}demon woman{/i}, or a {i}succubus{/i} for that freaking matter. I don't sleep around {i}that{/i} much.
Hm, guess that'd be number three, it's rude to call someone names they don't like, such as freakishly strong.

Fourthly, some guys just have no backbone, not my fault they can't handle me wanting them to try and keep up with me, or some {i}friendly{/i} competition, I mean, it's no fun if the guy doesn't at least {i}try{/i} to compete with me. It's not like I'm forcing anyone into a drinking competition though hehe...
It's just I'd like to, {i}enjoy{/i} myself too. You know?
Fifthly, sure I can be a little high strung, a tad aggressive, and just a little bit boisterous and loud, but that's not that off-putting, it's not like I'm incoherently violent or something.
Elena clearly either didn't hear Elly, or is ignoring her comment.

{i}Lastly{/i} just because I'm getting on in years, does {i}not{/i} mean I'm hopelessly {i}desperate{/i} to get married. I'm just {i}highly{/i} interested in a long term relationship. A nice, simple, loving, monogamous relationship.
Actually I guess lastly would be I'm not going to be in some {i}harem.{/i} I especially don't want any monster girls involved. {i}Apparently{/i} that's a problem for a lot of guys, perverts...
I just want a normal happy marriage, where I get spoiled on occasion.
You glance over as you realize Elly has actually walked over from her table, book still in hand as she adjusts her glasses.
Elly, stop trying to crush my dreams.
I-I am not trying to crush your dreams sister!
Elly lets out a sigh as she continues.
I am just saying, with how the overall gender skew of the global population is, including monster girls. It's just, a {i}little{/i} bit selfish of you to consider that kind of relationship.
Yeah, and as I've said before, I'm perfectly fine with being selfish on this.
But the female population is at least three times that of the male population!
Eh, there {i}are{/i} monster girl lesbians. You can trim them off of that statistic. Plus most monsters live longer, they can wait longer too.
Just because monster girls make up most of that population metric doesn't mean you can try and just ignore portions of it!

Sure I can, they're still {i}technically{/i} the enemy anyways. And seriously, we need to keep the human race going if we're going to have general peace between the species. Speaking of Elly we should start trying to find you a boyfriend sometime.
I keep telling you that I don't have time to with my studies, not to mention- {i}hey!{/i} Don't try to switch the topic!

Well, it's not like you're going to change my mind on the matter anyways. I'm not going to let reality crush my dreams of romance. But I swear monster girls are trying to steal away all the good men...
Elly lets out a tired sigh and starts to slowly march back to her table.
You could try a little harder to compete with them you know...
Pft, {i}how,{/i} just be a depraved seductress?
Hgk- {i}I meant drink less!{/i} Maybe be less stubborn too.
{i}Oh.{/i} Yeah, that would make more sense coming from you.

Elly lets out one more exhausted sigh and plops back down at her table, shoving her face back into her book. Elena meanwhile turns back to you with a smile, and you feel like you never really had a chance to get a word in edgewise that whole 'conversation'.
Anyways [ThePlayerName]. Anything else you need to talk about.

EventText.NameOfScene: Talk about other people in town.

theScene: Hm? Elly is the guild's Archmage, she handles all of the guildhall's magical dealings such as teaching magic and arcane research.
She also uses her magic to easily keep the guildhall spotless and makes all of the magic resources we give out like skill tomes, recall stones, and the grimoires. When we run out at least.
Really she spends most of her time researching Lucidia, studying artifacts, and pursuing her personal magic studies, usually to help improve things around here.
She also helped me set up a proper organization system for all the records and files we have.
I handle dealing with all the adventurers, checking over the quest postings, managing all the guilds supplies, sorting out reward money, and piles of {i}paperwork{/i}.
She practically spits out the last bit with barely veiled disgust before continuing on with a smile.
Both of us handle different ends of teaching skills too, so overall we make an amazing team, even now when we're not out in the field adventuring ourselves.
She leans forward and drops her voice to a low whisper.

Seriously though, she could never handle talking with all the people I do on a daily basis, I think she'd either start drinking more than me, or go crazy. Not to mention she's not exactly great with crowds either, and she isn't always very tactful with her words, or keeping her emotions in check when it comes to specific things.
She lets out a wistful sigh as she leans back.
Hpm, this place would be a total wreck if even {i}one{/i} of us was missing.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
Elena blinks hard and looks at you with mild bafflement.
You give her a nod in the affirmative.
Elena lets out a sigh of mild defeat before continuing.
Honestly I get stuck in 'business' mode sometimes and don't really even think about it half the time when I'm talking about her. We're more like co-workers on the clock than siblings.
She gives you a small smile as she continues talking.

This definitely isn't the first time it's slipped my mind either, me and Elly have already talked about it, so don't worry there isn't any kind of weird {i}'oh she's no sister of mine'{/i} kind of crap.

But it's not like we have as much time to spend together anymore, our jobs are very time-consuming. Even when we do have time off together we both have very different interests, she hates going to the bar, for example, the {i}not{/i}-magic books she reads aren't exactly something I'd enjoy reading with her, and neither of us are really into board games anymore.

The two of us have done a lot of sisterly adventuring together back home, and work aside I don't think either of us are {i}exactly{/i} keen on going adventuring in Lucidia. Neither of us {i}really{/i} want to be in the know of the other's sex life.
Elena lets out a content sigh before continuing.
But even though we both have very different interests and live different lives despite being so close, we still chat and talk to each other.

Oh! And if you decide to date my sister, and break her heart, I will make your life a living hell. Either through bureaucracy or by repeatedly throwing you into the ocean. Despite the enchantment covering the continent, I want to assure you, I would find a way.
Her business cheer voice is slightly edged with just enough of a threatening tone that you can definitely tell she's serious. Thankfully she quickly returns to her normal businesslike cheer.
But you don't seem like the type to be meanspirited or dumb enough to do something like that.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.

Amber's not exactly the most fun person to talk to, but runs the best adventuring supply store in town, and she carries {i}everything{/i} you need, from potions, to equipment, and even {i}'weapons'.{/i}
A smile tugs at Elena's calm face as she brings up her last point.

Sex toys aside she does {i}actually{/i} sell a few ornamental weapons to show off in display cases, and even equipment maintenance. Personally I love keeping my old adventuring gear in top shape, {i}just{/i} in case.
An excited and giddy smile spreads over Elena's face as she thinks about it.
Okay, maybe I just {i}really{/i} love my great-sword. Hahhh...♪ {i}Oh!{/i} Amber also takes custom orders for weapons too.
Elena awkwardly coughs as her prior excitement vanishes.
F-For the display weapons, not the sex toys.
She smoothly shifts the topic with a business smile.
The guild and her store have a good contract set up for handling most of our supplies. So I can assure you despite her, well, sour attitude, she is {i}very{/i} reliable.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
Ugh, that woman rubs me the wrong way. And no that is {i}not{/i} a sex joke.
Her cold stare and even colder tone sends a shiver through you. You get the feeling she's had this conversation before.
Well, as you know she runs the town's only Inn. Which the guild has a deal set up to allow adventurers to stay at free of charge. But it doubles as a {i}brothel{/i} for monster girls to rent humans.
Elena starts to quickly mutter under her breath a little awkwardly.
She also has the best bar in town. But that's besides the point.
Sure Vivian's all {i}nice{/i}, with her {i}'oh so perfect'{/i} body, all thanks to the {i}Goddess.{/i} Not to mention her {i}oooh, I'm all mysterious and subtle{/i}. Egh.

She's clearly up to something, that being she's up to being a {i}horrible{/i} match-maker. Seriously she's set me up with at least {i}10{/i} different people and none of them worked out in the {i}slightest.{/i}

If she wasn't trying to set up {i}everyone{/i} in town I'd swear that whole brothel operation she has going was just to try and steer men to only be interested in monster girls... She's probably just trying to make men into perverts or something {i}dumb{/i} like that.
There's a bit of a pause in the conversation while Elena mutters darkly to herself before speaking up again.
Hmph, despite what Vivian claims, donating to the church doesn't {i}even{/i} make you prettier or anything like that? ...Not that I've tried it personally.
She awkwardly trails off before coughing and shifting the topic a little.
The point is [ThePlayerName], don't trust her at face value, be careful of the quests she posts, and you better not work at her brothel.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
Lillian? She... {i}Means{/i} well. Just never tell her something you want to keep secret.
Elena lets out a tired sigh.
She does an excellent job running the church though, and I don't think I've ever {i}actually{/i} seen her run out of energy.
Though she has a bit of a one-track mind sometimes... Like when she was entirely convinced Elly was a witch. I'm so glad that stopped...
I just wish she would be a little more lenient about who she lets into the church. She actually forces me to button up my top more or wear a coat over this. It's already so {i}restrictive.{/i}
Elena lets out another sigh before letting a small smile spread over her face.

Hehe, honestly, I'm pretty sure she's so adverse to more 'erotic' things because she actually grew up in this town. The church specifically too, I think. I've talked to her about it before, and I even have a few old town records about it. Really it would also explain why she's so strict with who she even lets work at the church.
We definitely had a few {i}weirdos{/i} try to set up there.
But thankfully Lillian knows how to put her foot down.
Elena's smile briefly shifts to a smirk as she recalls something, but she doesn't say.
Heh, despite being stubborn, a bit {i}too{/i} preachy, and a tad too much of a blabbermouth... I still like her. Really, it's hard to {i}dis{/i}like her.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
Oh! I'd say Belle is one of my best friends. She's a great listener and an even better bartender.
Though we don't really hang out other than when I'm at the bar.
Heh, she works just as much as I do I think.
{i}Though{/i} I do feel bad she has to deal with me drunk most of the time too, I'm uh, not {i}exactly{/i} on my best behavior when I'm like that.
Hmm... Honestly talking about her makes me wonder what I should get to drink tonight.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
How do your records keep so up to date on my progress?: 1
Shouldn't there be more people working here?: 3
Do you teach any normal combat skills?: 3
Isn't that outfit a bit too revealing for a guild uniform?: 3

EventText.NameOfScene: Step away from the guild counter.
theScene: Okay! Have a nice day, and good luck adventuring!
